content.problemStatement:
Merge sort is a divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that divides the unsorted list into smaller sublists, sorts those sublists, and then merges them back together to produce a sorted list. 

Here's the step-by-step algorithm for the merge sort:
- If the list contains only one element or is empty, it is already sorted.
- Divide the unsorted list into two equal halves.
   - Recursively sort the left and right halves using merge sort.
   - Merge the two sorted halves to produce a single sorted list.
   - Compare the first element of both halves.
   - Move the smaller element to the merged list.
   - Repeat the comparison and moving process until one of the halves is empty.
   - Copy any remaining elements from the non-empty half to the merged list.
   - The merged list is now a sorted list.

### Task
Merge sort is a relatively complex algorithm! \
Go through the code given in the IDE to understand the steps. \
Click on Submit to continue.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
    - The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$ — the number of elements in the array originally.
    - The next line contains $N$ space-separated integers — $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$, which are the original array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of elements that she has to remove from the array.
